skill.name: page-cro
skill.description: Analyze page-level conversion performance with a structured CRO score, prioritized recommendations, and testable hypotheses for marketing pages.
skill.summary: Diagnose conversion blockers and plan evidence-based page improvements.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged weak cryptography at SKILL.md:3, SKILL.md:161, and SKILL.md:272, plus system reconnaissance at SKILL.md:141. Review found these are false positives from ordinary Markdown and CRO prose, not executable code or operational instructions. No evidence found for network access, command execution, data exfiltration, prompt injection, or malicious intent.

low_findings.title: Static Weak Cryptography Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The flagged lines are description metadata, a Failure modes heading, and CRO guidance about design effort. They do not reference cryptographic algorithms or implement cryptography.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines contain only Markdown prose and no executable code. The apparent matches are substrings in normal words, not cryptographic usage.

low_findings.title: Static System Reconnaissance Alert Is a False Positive
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The flagged line lists paid traffic context for CRO analysis. It does not request system information, host discovery, or environment inspection.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is a marketing traffic-source prompt inside a CRO framework. There is no command, tool call, or instruction to inspect the local system.

content.value_statement: Many marketing pages lose conversions because core blockers are unclear. This skill scores the page and turns CRO issues into prioritized fixes.
actual_capabilities: Scores page readiness from 0 to 100 across six CRO categories.
Classifies pages as high, moderate, low, or not conversion-ready.
Diagnoses value proposition, CTA hierarchy, trust signals, objections, and UX friction.
Prioritizes quick wins and high-impact improvements by conversion constraint.
Creates measurable hypotheses for experiments when testing is appropriate.
Suggests headline, subheadline, and CTA alternatives when useful.
limitations: Does not guarantee conversion lifts or revenue gains.
Requires page content, screenshots, analytics, or traffic context for stronger recommendations.
Does not run experiments, deploy changes, or configure analytics tools.
Recommends fixing fundamentals before testing low-readiness pages.

use_cases.title: Audit a Landing Page
use_cases.description: Review a campaign landing page for message match, CTA focus, proof, and friction before launching paid traffic.

use_cases.title: Improve a Pricing Page
use_cases.description: Find pricing objections, trust gaps, and unclear next steps that may reduce trial or demo conversions.

use_cases.title: Refresh a Homepage
use_cases.description: Clarify positioning, audience routing, and conversion paths before investing in redesign or experiments.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Page Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner audit with page copy or screenshot notes.
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this page for conversion readiness. Score it from 0 to 100 and list the biggest blockers first.

prompt_templates.title: Landing Page Diagnosis
prompt_templates.scenario: Campaign page with known traffic source.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this landing page for paid traffic from [channel]. Check message match, CTA focus, trust, friction, and objections.

prompt_templates.title: Prioritized CRO Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Planning implementation work after a page audit.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a prioritized CRO plan for this page. Separate quick wins, structural improvements, and testable hypotheses with success metrics.

prompt_templates.title: Advanced Experiment Brief
prompt_templates.scenario: Preparing tests for a page with enough traffic.
prompt_templates.prompt: Build an experiment brief for this page. Include the readiness score, testing eligibility, hypotheses, variants, rationale, and primary metric.

output_examples.input: A SaaS pricing page with many plan details and a low trial signup rate.
output_examples.output: The skill returns a readiness score, identifies price-value objections, flags CTA hierarchy issues, and suggests proof near decision points.

output_examples.input: A campaign landing page for paid search traffic promoting a free consultation.
output_examples.output: The skill checks traffic-message match, highlights missing trust signals, recommends copy changes, and defines a measurable test hypothesis.

output_examples.input: A blog post that should drive newsletter signups.
output_examples.output: The skill reviews CTA placement, contextual relevance, reader intent, and low-friction signup options without treating the post like a sales page.

best_practices: Provide the page URL, screenshot, copy, target audience, traffic source, and primary conversion goal.
Include baseline conversion data and past experiment notes when available.
Use the readiness score to decide whether to fix fundamentals or run tests.
anti_patterns: Asking for random button color changes without a conversion constraint.
Running A/B tests when the value proposition or primary CTA is unclear.
Optimizing several conversion goals at once on a single page.

faq.question: What pages does this skill support?
faq.answer: It supports homepages, landing pages, pricing pages, feature pages, product pages, and content pages with CTAs.

faq.question: Does it guarantee a higher conversion rate?
faq.answer: No. It provides diagnosis, prioritization, and testable recommendations, but performance depends on traffic and execution.

faq.question: Can it replace analytics or user research?
faq.answer: No. It works best with analytics, heatmaps, recordings, surveys, and customer context.

faq.question: When should I avoid A/B testing?
faq.answer: Avoid testing when traffic is too low, the goal is unclear, or the readiness score is below 70.

faq.question: What inputs improve the audit quality?
faq.answer: Share the page content, screenshot, audience, source traffic, current conversion rate, and known objections.

faq.question: Can it write copy alternatives?
faq.answer: Yes. It can suggest headline, subheadline, and CTA alternatives with rationale tied to user intent.
